description: The Newton Gang was an outlaw group active from around 1919 to 1924, primarily engaged in train and bank robberies across the United States and Canada. Comprising four Newton brothers and an associate, they claimed to have robbed 75 banks and six trains without killing anyone. Their most famous heist was the 1924 Rondout train robbery in Illinois, netting $3 million. The gang's later years saw involvement with the Barrow Gang and continued criminal activity into the 1970s.

country_links.evidence_quote: The gang, including all four Newton brothers and Brent Glasscock, went on to rob a series of trains and banks in 10 states and Canada.
country_links.context: The gang robbed a series of trains and banks in Canada.
